title: Calendar Widget - Label By Multiple Column Types
excerpt:
Description

posts.author: krasten (Kristen Harris)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I have a calendar widget with three connected boards. I need to be able to individually choose each board’s “Label By” option in the widget settings. The system only allows me to label by one column type at a time. I want to be able to choose the label that makes the most sense for each board so that our team sees the context they need at a glance without having to completely re-do the structures of the three boards.

Seems like a simple thing to allow, in my opinion.

What are you trying to achieve

The widget compiles important dates for our department. One board tracks trainings, one tracks conferences, and the other birthdays & work anniversaries. In order to get the widget to display “John Doe | Birthday” or “Jane Doe | Work Anniversary” on the calendar, I have to label by Column Name. Whereas conferences, I need labelled by Group Name so that it displays the conference details along with the employee name.

posts.author: reinier (Reinier ― 📅 Calendar Plus 🍅 Automato)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Kristen,

I never realised this was a limitation! Voted!

If you’re open to marketplace apps, you can work around this with Calendar Plus. You can pick different labels for every date column you’re displaying.

–
